column2: Captain!
Nevermind her, boy. Help me up.
(Magda kneels down to investigate the dust left in the shape of the knight's body.)
Magda?
Something's wrong, right? The air feels heavy.
Shh... I hear something.
(Magda easily finds the marble buried in the pile.)
Here, hold this.
(He takes it the glass bead.)
Ugh. It feels grime-y. Kind of tacky?
Just hold on to it, Lachlan. Your uniform has pockets, this dress is all belts, no utility.
Sister, if you would.
Could you heal me? My ankle got injured during the fall.
column7: >maybe rachelle catches josephine in all other routes, so she's not injured?

column2: I assure you, I'm no use limping.
She's kinda terrible at prayer magic-- Oof!
(Magda pulls her elbow back from where she hit him.)
I would be happy to oblige, my lady.
Thank you.
(Magda crouches next to the lady and holds her hand over the swollen joint.)
(Lachlan winces like he's bracing for a blow.)
(Magda's hand glows brightly, filling the room with divine light.)
(When her hand moves, the ankle is healed.)
Whoah, you're good! I've never seen a Sister do it that fast.
I'VE never seen you do it that fast.
Um. Maybe we're close to the Guardian Deity down here?
I'm glad I can entrust my safety to you, then, Sister...
Magda. You can just call me Magda.
A pleasant name. You may address me as Duchess Josephine.
I'm Einar, also a knight of House Ronan.
And I'm Lachlan.
(Magda turns to give Lachlan a foul look.)
Sorry. Just seemed like we were all introducing ourselves.
(Her eyes shine with interest.)
You're a mage of the Academy? I don't recognize you, but your uniform is quite... large for your frame.
What do you specialize in?
I'm really only good at--
He's very adept with fire. He's one of my wards, I'll manage him.
Right, yeah.
Alright. After you, then, Sister Magda. I imagine we have to find a way out of here next.
